programme.title: Belly Of A Nation 3
programme.desc: It's been a year and some hawkers prospered while others had to consolidate. On the anniversary of their New York branch, the hawkers have to decide whether to stay or go.

programme.title: MH370 A Decade On
programme.desc: 10 years since the disappearance of MH370 how are the family members of the victims coping and are we any closer to finding the missing plane?

programme.title: Belly Of A Nation 3
programme.desc: Obscure foods and humble snacks are making a comeback. Meet the little-known Nasi Ulam, see how the Tamil Vadai became Singaporean and appreciate the many cultural interpretations of the curry puff.

programme.title: Belly Of A Nation 3
programme.desc: We look at rice and its binding power as family members come together to produce the various dishes that are based on the many forms of the grain, from Nasi Rawon to Cantonese Cheung Fun.

programme.title: Singapore Hour - Episode 37 - 1/4-01
programme.desc: Singapore Hour - Episode 37 - 1/4-01

programme.title: Belly Of A Nation 3
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 New York, New York
programme.desc: We explore the Indian curry puff, Karipap and the couple who make this at a hawker stall. And for the first time in New York City, we see how three Singapore hawkers are making their presence felt.

programme.title: Belly Of A Nation 3
programme.desc: It's been a year and some hawkers prospered while others had to consolidate. On the anniversary of their New York branch, the hawkers have to decide whether to stay or go.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation.

programme.title: Africa Live
programme.desc: Africa Live is a news program that focuses on African issues and issues related to this diverse continent. CGTN's Nairobi-based broadcast center produces Africa Live.

programme.title: Talk Africa
programme.desc: Talk Africa is a weekly discussion show that brings the most influential voices in Africa to the world.

programme.title: Sports Scene
programme.desc: Sports Scene brings you closer to all the action from all corners of the world, with highlights from across Europe, North America and China's top sports leagues.

programme.title: Biz Talk
programme.desc: A groundbreaking 30-minute interview show, giving an in-depth look into how top companies around the world conduct business and how policymakers view the fast-changing world economy. It presents 360 degree profiles of industry movers and shakers, tech mavericks and policymakers, and drills down on the stories behind their success, to give you clarity on what’s happening in every facet of business.

programme.title: The World Today
programme.desc: The World Today is the core rolling service of CGTN. keep viewers up-to-date with all the world's top stories as they happen.

programme.title: Travelogue
programme.desc: Travelogue is a 30-minute travel program. Through the journeys of our intrepid hosts, we experience the beauty, culture and mystery of China, discovering how people live in this vast country and showcasing China's transformation to the world.

programme.title: Leaders Talk

They will share their insights on the major events shaping today’s world, providing an international viewpoint to understand our times, the visions we share, the challenges we face and the efforts we can make for a better future.

programme.title: Inheritors
programme.desc: Inheritors is a multi-media product from CGTN that shines a light on China’s rich Intangible Cultural Heritage. Each 30-minute TV edition is hosted by a long-term expatriate in China who has a unique understanding of the country’s culture. He or she will introduce viewers to people who are dedicating their lives to its protection, inheritance, innovation, development and promotion.

programme.title: Leaders Talk
programme.desc: Leaders Talk is China Media Group’s new current affairs program featuring one-on-one interviews with heads of state and high-ranking government officials and heads of international organizations. They will share their insights on the major events shaping today’s world, providing an international viewpoint to understand our times, the visions we share, the challenges we face and the efforts we can make for a better future.

programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already "living in the future"!

programme.title: Digits
programme.desc: We peer inside "The Internet of Things" and reveal how web advertising, search engines and 24/7 surveillance have transformed our lives.

programme.title: Digits
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Guardians Of The Web
programme.desc: Muller introduces viewers to "white" hackers, the U.S. Secret Service’s cybercrime division including West Point soldiers working to protect us from the Web.

programme.title: Rapidly Evolving Human with Spencer Wells
programme.desc: Renowned geneticist and author Spencer Wells reveals how changes in our genetic code have fueled major changes in our appearance and capabilities over time.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E27 Path to the Pros
programme.desc: What does it take to become a professional tennis player? Both on and off the court preparations help build the strength of character and focused mind to take on the challenges of a sport that demands precision and superior athleticism.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Increasingly high-tech tools are being used to evaluate youth baseball players. The question is can they outperform professional scouts?

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Firefighters have relied on the same basic tools for decades. But former NASA engineer Mike Ralston hopes to change that with MSTAR – a new breed of an emergency vehicle that brings a powerful suite of high-tech tools from Silicon Valley to the front lines of firefighting.

programme.title: Breakthrough
programme.desc: On July 5, 1996, scientists cloned a sheep, Dolly. Now genetics have advanced to gene editing. Promising to pave the way for curing genetically carried illnesses.

programme.title: Digits
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Connecting To The Future
programme.desc: Host Derek Muller escorts us to the physical heart of the Internet, and demonstrates how Internet co-creators Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn devised a language.

programme.desc: Top trappers from Florida Fish and Wildlife go to battle each day against dangerous nuisance alligators that lurk beneath the lush lakes and canals of Palm Beach.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E73 Mother Of The Everglades
programme.desc: Strap-in for a breathtaking tour of the history and wildlife of Lake Okeechobee, one of the largest freshwater lakes in the United States, and the source of life for South Florida's magnificent Everglades.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Should we save parasites? These species have a bad reputation but are an essential part of healthy ecosystems throughout the world.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Growing up in Montana, Casey Anderson came to love and appreciate wild animals at a very young age. He found a particular kinship with bears. One day he was faced with a fateful decision, leave and orphaned grizzly cub to die in the wild or save it. Now he is faced with that same decision again.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Follow a family of sperm whales in the heart of the Indian Ocean. Alongside a team of scientists, who have witnessed their most intimate moments over the past 7 years, we will lift the veil on the largest toothed predator on earth.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.sub-title: S01 : E30 Chungungo: The World's Smallest Otter
programme.desc: Meet a family of Chungungos, the world's mysterious, smallest otter. Living along the untamed coast of the southern Pacific Ocean, these furry, intelligent, and adorable creatures make their home in the wild of Chile.

programme.title: Bright Now
programme.desc: Take a journey with famed underwater photographer Peter Kragh, as he explores the coast of California, and reveals some of the most spectacular life found under the sea.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Join ecologist Karen Odendaal as she gives us a tour of the Manyoni Private Game Reserve in the heart of Zululand, South Africa.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Throw on your wetsuit, and join filmmakers Kyle McBurnie and Alex Warham as they show off their California wild backyard, deep in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Take a trip to the north of Mexico with our host, Eréndira Hernández, as she shows off one of the most extraordinary landscapes in the Americas.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: The hustle and bustle of New York City is legendary worldwide, but right in its backyard lies a serene natural haven that most know very little about.

programme.title: My Wild Backyard
programme.desc: Meet Rene Araneda, a wildlife filmmaker and Patagonia explorer, as he takes you on a tour of his favorite place in the world: Torres del Paine.

programme.title: Battle For the Moon
programme.desc: After the Gemini program, America embarked on the Apollo program.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Earthquakes baffle experts as they try to gather the information necessary to predict the next big one.

programme.title: Lake Geneva: The Giant Tsunami
programme.desc: In 563 AD, Geneva was destroyed by a tsunami, and it could happen again. Scientists are working to assess the danger and prepare for disaster.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Venus: Death of a Planet
programme.desc: While nature bestowed Earth with life, it enveloped deadly Venus in sulfuric acid clouds and volcanic flows.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: The Cassini spacecraft's observations of Saturn's moons showed them to be unique worlds and revealed new data.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 The Largest Black Holes in the Universe
programme.desc: Black holes are mysterious. Find out what impact the massive giants have had on the Universe and its galaxies.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E07 Voyager: Journey to the Stars
programme.desc: What will we learn about our Universe from the Voyager spacecraft's epic journey to the stars?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E08 The Search for Earth-Like Planets
programme.desc: Is our Earth one of the countless life-bearing worlds in our huge galaxy, or is it alone in a barren universe?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 The Riddle of Antimatter
programme.desc: How did matter come to exist, and what became of its birth twin, a mysterious substance called antimatter?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E10 Super Hurricanes and Typhoons
programme.desc: Why do some hurricanes and typhoons evolve into monsters that leave in their wake trails of destruction?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Mysteries of a Dark Universe
programme.desc: Cosmologists were stunned to learn the universe is flying apart in all directions at an ever-increasing rate.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Relive the harrowing moments in Launch Control during NASA's second "Return to Flight" test flight .

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: As the clock ticks toward the critical Mars launch window, NASA scrambles to fix a critical space mistake.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Despite multiple pressures, a shocking discovery causes NASA to cancel a historic launch.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 The End of Genesis
programme.desc: A spacecraft freefalls from orbit smashing into the ground when its parachute fails to deploy.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: The loss of Space Shuttle Columbia stands as one of the worst disasters in spaceflight history.

programme.title: 13 Factors that Saved Apollo 13
programme.desc: Apollo 13 suffers an oxygen leak that leaves the spacecraft with limited power.

programme.desc: Black holes are mysterious. Find out what impact the massive giants have had on the Universe and its galaxies.

programme.title: Connections Between Science & Philosophy
programme.desc: Dr. Menas Kafatos is internationally known for bridging the disciplines, writing andlecturing extensively on the convergence between science and philosophy, as there isan inherent need for a dialogue between science, spirituality, and religion. His book, Creating Cosmos with Deepak Chopra discusses the melding of topics like inflation,environmental issues, natural hazards, quantum theory and consciousness.

programme.title: Iranian Space Pioneer
programme.desc: Iranian electrical engineer, entrepreneur and former astronaut, Anousheh Ansari wasthe first female private space explorer to fly to the International Space Station. Sheexplains the challenging journey and the determination needed to reach space afterimmigrating to the U.S. from Iran. She also became the first person to publish a blogfrom space. The content invited readers to share her experience with the world. Shedescribed the Russian Soyuz capsule during liftoff, talked about the sights and smells ofthe space station, and explained the intricacies of everyday activities.

programme.title: Philosopher of the Mind
programme.desc: Dr. Paavo Pylkk�nen�s eclectic approach combines new physics-based insights withthose of analytical philosophy, phenomenology, cognitive science and neuroscience. Heproposes a view in which the mechanistic framework of classical physics andneuroscience is complemented by a more holistic approach. Pylkk�nen is particularlyknown for his work on mind-body-studies, building on David Bohm's interpretation ofquantum mechanics.

programme.title: Evolution of Ideas
programme.desc: Everything we can know about our lives and the universe is a subjective experience,either formed by one's own perceptions or the reports of others. Dr. Bernardo Kastrupexplains how we commonly associate this understanding about the universe assomething separate of our consciousness, however he strongly believes otherwise;Kastrup believes the evolution of ideas we perceive about the universe are actuallyoriginating within us.

programme.title: Book Of Knowledge
programme.desc: Traveling the world 30+ years, David Verdesi finds himself back in Rome. Urged to uncover thereason behind the instinctive human desire for spiritual yearning, Verdesi starts retracing theintersection of science and theology within the city his family has called home since the early1400s.Vibrating from the walls of the city�s sacred spaces, he discovers findings of oral history,predating the beginning of humanity by thousands of years. Documents and rare rituals thatupend conventional ideas of the behaviors of the mind in ancient times. Narratives heavilyinfluenced by civilizations and cultures beyond Rome, continue to influence the moral compassof life today, Verdesi realizes he...

programme.title: Across the Ice
programme.desc: After 45+ expeditions throughout over 150+ countries, one of the world's leadingadventurers and visionaries of conservation, Swedish explorer Johan Ernst Nilsondawns upon his biggest adventure yet. Follow his journey across the planet from theNorth Pole to the South Pole in Pole2Pole. The 525 day mostly carbon-neutral trekcovers 20,000 miles by means of skis, dogsled, boat, bike and kite. Pole2Pole is notonly one of the most difficult adventures he�s ever attempted, but also one of his mostphilanthropic adventures. Johan visits charities along the way to share their stories andraise global awareness.

programme.title: Chasing Bliss
programme.desc: Water is life, and the great conductor that connects us all. The oceans cover over 70%of our planet and hold almost 97% of the earth's water. True bliss in constant movementgiving life around the globe. This is a story of an eclectic mix of people across Hawaii,Ecuador and the USA, who draw energy from this life force and channel it positively intotheir everyday lives. Join Paulino in understanding what drives Surfer Laid Hamilton, orfilmmaker Robert Greaves, Big-Wave World Champion Paige Alms and WindsurfingWorld Record Holder Sarah Hauser. Coffee Farmer Anti Clancy, Dr Greg Mitchell,Research Scientist & Algae Visionary at Scripps.

programme.title: Particle Physics
programme.desc: At only 21, Frank Wilczek, a graduate student at Princeton University, defined theproperties of color gluons, which hold atomic nuclei together while working with DavidGross. He is known, among other things, for the discovery of asymptotic freedom, thedevelopment of quantum chromodynamics, the invention of axions, and the discoveryand exploitation of new forms of quantum statistics (anyons).

programme.title: Consciousness of Culture
programme.desc: Bruce Parry highlights the moments of his life that formed his career and life passion tochampion indigenous cultures around the world through exploration and film. Bruce isinterested in altered states of mind, and the creation of myths, why do we think what wethink? And what life phenomena and environments sculpt these ideas?

programme.title: The Nature of All Things
programme.desc: What is it that makes things what they are? Where does it come from? For ProfessorSeyyed Hossein Nasr, the quest for knowledge, specifically knowledge which enablesman to understand the true nature of things and which furthermore, �liberates anddelivers him from the fetters and limitations of earthly existence,� has been (andcontinues to be) the central concern and determinant of how we understand and live ourlives.

programme.title: Astrology Zone
programme.desc: Susan Miller is the founder and astrologist for AstrologyZone.com. The trusted,twenty-year-old platform provides the latest in intelligent astrological trends. Thisdiscussion with Deepak Chopra breaks down many popular misconceptions aboutastrological predictions, the various disciplines of study, ego, and consciousness inrelation to astrology. Miller also outlines the golden rule to understanding and getting themost out of astrological predications: astrology shows the condition, but not theoutcome�that outcome is completely up to you. �I can give you questions to ask to helpyou decide, but in the end, you write the script of your life.�

programme.title: Modern Metaphysical Idealism
programme.desc: Dr. Bernardo Kastrup, philosopher and author has been leading the modernrenaissance of metaphysical idealism. His theoretical reflections on the mind explainconsciousness as the only element of life that allows us to understand reality, andeverything we know about reality is subject to our experiences. This idea is often refutedby the thought that the brain is the source, not images and experiences provided byconsciousness.

programme.title: Nature, Space and Matter
programme.desc: Our understanding of the nature of time, space and matter have changed�even whatwe mean by these terms�in just the last few decades. Our interpretations continue tochange and more theories form as we learn and study these topics. Dr. Fred Alan Wolfexplains the origins of many of these foundational theories that have formed at differenttimes and why they came about. Before quantum physics, people knew that humanbeings were conscious. We knew that animals were conscious. But now, with newrevelations in quantum physics we begin to see a new role for consciousness.

programme.title: Creation Is Constant
programme.desc: Duane Elgin wants to show that the universe is a living field of existence, and that weare always in communion with a field of aliveness whether we are conscious of it or not.Throughout this lecture, Elgin explains his belief that in a living system, each part isintegral to the whole, so each entity is intimately connected to the entire universe. Tohim, creation is not something that happened with the Big Bang. Instead, creation isconsistently forming around us from a deeper abyss. The universe�s existence isbrought moment by moment through this continuous, moving process.

programme.title: Consciousness & Healing
programme.desc: Dr. Shamini Jain conducts clinical research in the areas of psychoneuroimmunology andintegrative medicine, receiving several awards from scientific organizations for her work.She�s been continuously interested in the process of healing and strived to uncovermany of the doubts she had in undergraduate school when she discovered the fieldpsychoneuroimmunology; the integrated relationship between mind, brain and body.

programme.title: Borderline Consciousness
programme.desc: Berit Brogaard was the first researcher to show that consciousness comes in degreesand that there can be borderline cases of consciousness. Her areas of research includeperception, consciousness, emotions, philosophical psychology, semantics andphilosophical logic. She also researches synesthesia, savant syndrome, blind sight, andperceptual reports.

programme.title: The Knobe Effect
programme.desc: We have experiences, creativity, understanding, thoughts, feelings and desires but dowe know how these occurrences happen? Joshua Knobe�s research has beenconcerned with the impact of people's moral judgments on their intuitions about thisquestion. He believes strongly that people's intuitions about whether a particular entity isconscious, essentially depends on whether the entity has the right sort of body.Therefore, he believes embodiment shapes our view of what is reality.

programme.title: Full Circle
programme.sub-title: S02 : E01 Spotlight on Fashion: Circular Economy Show: CNMI Sustainable Fashion
programme.desc: Delve into the world of fashion and head to the Camera Nazionale della Moda (CNMI) Sustainable Fashion Awards 2022, in Milan!

programme.title: Protecting Our Future
programme.desc: Meet five innovators, conservationists and social entrepreneurs making a positive difference across five nations.

programme.title: Quantifying Beauty
programme.desc: How do we scientifically quantify beauty in people and in nature? How does the worldembody beautiful ideas? Dr. Frank Wilczek�s conversation with Deepak Chopradiscusses his book, �A Beautiful Question� which tests the common misconceptionsabout how we form our opinions on success, beauty and our imaginations.

programme.title: World Heritages in Handan
programme.desc: Handan, a renowned historical and cultural city in China, boasts abundant cultural and tourism resources, including the Handan Museum and Congtai Park in Congtai District, as well as the Xiangtangshan Grottoes, Guangfu Ancient City, Cizhou Kiln, etc. Handan also has a long-standing connection with the Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal, a world cultural heritage. As a great project created in ancient China, the Grand Canal has not only exerted a profound impact on cities along the route in terms of transportation, economy and culture, but also served as an important driving force for Handan's development.

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing
programme.desc: Join us in southwest China's Chongqing, one of the world's biggest cities. From futuristic skylines and cyberpunk streets, to fire-breathing opera, spicy hotpot and kungfu tea, we're diving deep into this megacity the size of Austria.

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing
programme.desc: Join us in southwest China's Chongqing, one of the world's biggest cities. From futuristic skylines and cyberpunk streets, to fire-breathing opera, spicy hotpot and kungfu tea, we're diving deep into this megacity the size of Austria.

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing
programme.desc: Join us in southwest China's Chongqing, one of the world's biggest cities. From futuristic skylines and cyberpunk streets, to fire-breathing opera, spicy hotpot and kungfu tea, we're diving deep into this megacity the size of Austria.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Quanzhou Nanyin: A Living Fossil in the History of Chinese Music
programme.desc: Nanyin, also known as “Quanzhou Nanyin,” is an ancient Han Chinese musical tradition whose singing style preserves the time-honored ethnic vocal techniques predating the Tang Dynasty, earning it the title of “a living fossil in the history of Chinese music.”The program features Yegor, a Ukrainian music enthusiast, visiting Master Su Shuyong, a Nanyin inheritor, to hear her decades-long stories of teaching this art. They also tour the Quanzhou Nanyin Troupe, where, led by its artistic director Zhuang Lifen, they learn about the basic performance formats and instruments of Nanyin. Additionally, the episode follows a post-1990s Nanyin performer, Zheng Mingming, as she sips tea with guests...

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The Elegance of Luoyang
programme.desc: Luoyang, as the eastern starting point of the Silk Road, is a city with a rich history and a tradition of cultural inclusivity. As such, it enjoys a prominent position in the heritage of the whole world.In this episode, we’ll visit White Horse Temple to learn about its fascinating international background. At Longmen Grottoes, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year 2000, we’ll appreciate how the unique ancient splendor is being revived by 3D technology. At the city’s annual Peony Festival, we’ll explore the significance of China’s national flower and national dress.Invited to a Luoyang Water Banquet, we’ll learn something about the city’s culinary traditions....

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: The Elegance of Luoyang
programme.desc: Luoyang, as the eastern starting point of the Silk Road, is a city with a rich history and a tradition of cultural inclusivity. As such, it enjoys a prominent position in the heritage of the whole world.In this episode, we’ll visit White Horse Temple to learn about its fascinating international background. At Longmen Grottoes, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year 2000, we’ll appreciate how the unique ancient splendor is being revived by 3D technology. At the city’s annual Peony Festival, we’ll explore the significance of China’s national flower and national dress.Invited to a Luoyang Water Banquet, we’ll learn something about the city’s culinary traditions....

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing
programme.desc: Join us in southwest China's Chongqing, one of the world's biggest cities. From futuristic skylines and cyberpunk streets, to fire-breathing opera, spicy hotpot and kungfu tea, we're diving deep into this megacity the size of Austria.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.desc: Guarded by mountains in southeast China, Jiangxi channels rivers into Poyang Lake---the Yangtze's vital link. Starting from Mount Lu's misty slopes, watch and marvel as northern plains transition into an undulating land of hills and basins...

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port.With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads.In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites.Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its...

programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port.With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads.In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites.Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its...

After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port.With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads.In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites.Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its...

programme.title: My Little Cricket Fighter
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore a way of entertainment that has existed for thousands of years in China - "Cricket Fighting". Music, Chinese Chess, calligraphy and painting, keeping flowers, birds, fish and insects -- eight traditional hobbies of Chinese people. "keeping insects" is the last one, but it is unique to China. Although the insects are small, the related culture has been developed for thousands of years by Chinese people. It contains the unique cultural psychology, folk customs and aesthetic values of the Chinese people.

programme.title: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing
programme.desc: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing delves into the historical origins and cultural background of Fengdu Ghost City, and also features the Zhongyuan Festival celebrations and ancestor-worshipping rituals held here. These activities reflect the local people’s positive and optimistic outlook on life and death, as well as the great importance they attach to their ancestors, families and family traditions.

programme.title: China's Ancient “Wall Street”: Pingyao
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Pingyao – the ancient "Wall Street" of China and a historic banking hub. Enclosed by ancient city walls and seemingly frozen in time, this entire city, together with two nearby ancient temples, offers a one-of-a-kind adventure at this UNESCO World Heritage Site!

programme.title: Aerial China:Beijing
programme.desc: China’s capital of Beijing is a bucket list city for any self-styled world traveler, but let’s be honest, not all of us have the time! Not to worry, because we’re about to embark on an aerial journey over the nation’s heart, giving you a crash course on this massive hub of culture, international exchange, and technological innovation. If you’re afraid of heights, this may be the one time it’s okay to look down!

programme.title: Jingmai Wanderings: A Symphony of Humans and Tea
programme.desc: As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mount Jingmai embodies the ecological wisdom of forest–tea symbiosis and the rich cultural diversity of its ethnic communities. It plays a vital role in biodiversity conservation and the preservation of traditional tea culture.This episode follows the stories of representative individuals to explore three key themes — heritage preservation, ecological sustainability, and youth empowerment — revealing the unique charm and sustainable development model of Mount Jingmai.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: Seafaring Tales: Fu Ships
programme.desc: In past centuries, transporting goods along the Maritime Silk Road could be a hazardous undertaking. Ships often faced fierce storms which blew up on the high seas. To reduce the danger, China’s shipbuilders came up with a solution. They developed Fu ships, commonly known as junks, incorporating a special watertight bulkhead technology. Their revolutionary design allowed the ships to withstand the highest winds and roughest waters.The Maritime Silk Road was an ancient trading route that connected China to Southeast and South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. As a key passageway for trade and cultural exchange, it thrived for well over a thousand years, bringing Chinese tea, silk,...

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. There are no restrictions on the themes of the paintings, and there is no blessing they cannot convey. Yangliuqing New Year Paintings are loved all over the country for their beautiful details and vivid colors—but most of all, for the good wishes they impart to anyone who sees them.

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Greece, Euphoria Retreat
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, embark on a transformative journey to Euphoria Retreat.A mystical place offering a holistic approach to well-being aimed at restoring balance between body, mind and soul.After a long struggle with illness, its founder embarked on a personal quest to find balance. An in-depth reflection which resulted in the creation of a place where one could share and transmit this method of well-being. Euphoria Retreat is a clever blend of Taoist and Hellenic philosophy based on the symbolism of the five elements: water, wood, fire, earth and metal. Ancestral wisdom and scientific advances offer a transformative immersion in self-discovery. The tools developed by Euphoria...

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Italy, Palazzo Fiuggi
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset we discover Palazzo Fiuggi, a haven of well-being located in the heart of the natural springs of Fiuggi, famous for their purifying and healing powers.The Palazzo was born from the vision of a science and health enthusiast. This historic place of well-being offers exclusive medical-scientific therapies combining the healing properties of water with advances in Western medicine and alternative medicines. Upon arrival, a team of specialists carries out a targeted diagnosis to create a personalized protocol, rebalancing the mind, body and soul, with a simple promise: offering a longer and better-lived life.A constantly evolving method, between ancestral wisdom and...

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - The Banan Beach
programme.desc: Gabriela wakes up in a tent at Banan Beach in Dubai and gets inspired by Raha Moharrak the first Saudi women who climbed the Mount Everest and seven highest summits. Afterwards the two have kitesurf lessons and lots of fun. Gabriela ends her day with some dance flow yoga with her trainer Steph by sunset.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Poultry on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Poultry on the menu
programme.desc: Julie heads for Burgundy to discover ancient breeds of poultry reintroduced to France thanks to the work of breeder Fred Ménager. We'll then move on to Auvergne to discover how the famous Label Rouge is awarded and the role of certifying bodies ... because it's important to know what ends up on our plates. Julie also travels to the Drôme to cook guinea fowl, before heading to the Périgord to prepare a turkey stew. Julie will then head back up towards the center to discover an amazing fly maker who uses rooster feathers!

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Floating Yoga
programme.desc: Just Float, this is the latest yoga trend and mantra for an relaxed mind. As you enter a fully soundproof studio and float in vats filled with epson salts, you will disconnect from the urban buzz. That is Val’s promise as she takes us to experience this funny trend. With a sain mind comes a sain body: it’s time to head to Clover Juice, the most famous juice address in Los Angeles. As we make our way through Clover’s kitchen, we discover this way of life, mix and learn more on these beverage’s benefits.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Animal Farm
programme.desc: In this episode, we will go to a ranch, which is a real sanctuary for animals. The animals are king and the llama is the star. This center takes in animals to give them a safer and happier life in a beautiful setting. Then Val will participate in a Namastday Yoga session, another trend in yoga involving ropes hanging from the ceiling, acrobatic poses and great flexibility.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Planting vegetables in the fall
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Planting vegetables in the fall
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville, to continue installing vegetables in the vegetable garden. Yes, it is possible, even in autumn. Because if it's true that most vegetables are planted in spring or summer, some varieties can be planted in early autumn and are fast-growing, which will allow you to harvest them in late autumn or early winter. Let's get started! We're going to plant winter spinach together and we'll show you a little trick you need to know to harvest it properly.

programme.desc: In this episode, we put some color on your plate by preparing a rainbow bowl. For this you'll need quinoa, carrot, avocado, cucumber, radish, chioggia beet, pomegranate and buckwheat seeds, and mint. This bowl is composed by different colored vegetables which allows us to fill up with nutrients, minerals and vitamins. The look of a nice dish makes us secrete more digestive enzymes.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Claire
programme.desc: One woman’s personal mission to reduce her household rubbish turned into a passion that gave birth to Southeast Asia’s first Zero-Waste Bulk Food shop. Claire is a driven and successful mother of three who turned away from her corporate job to channel her business-savvy into reducing our impact on the environment and contributing to the improvement of her local community.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Zaira (Argentina)
programme.desc: For a long time Zaira let herself be defined solely as a survivor of the traumatic sexual assault that marked her life. In her healing search, she left behind the busy city life and moved to seek solace in the mountains of Mendoza. It is among these magnificent peaks that Zaira found peace within herself and harmony with her surroundings. Now, eager to help others heal, she is the leader of a trekking initiative that doubles as a support group for other women who are victims of sexual aggression.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Ile (Argentina)
programme.desc: Ileana studied business and worked long hours indoors for a multinational logistics company. Her love for dance and desire for movement drove her to quit her corporate life and begin a new chapter as a fitness trainer. Quickly exhausting the traditional methods available at her local gyms, she began to create innovative and groundbreaking exercise methods of her own. Her goal: to empower women by making them feel strong and feminine at the same time.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - So the great rivers may live - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 So the great rivers may live - Part 1
programme.desc: Water and its beauty, water and its power, water and its fragility... the water we all need is becoming scarce, through our own fault. Some rivers are dry, others are diverted, cut off by dams, others are simply polluted, and this for decades. We will discover how humans transformed the nature in Argentina, then in China and its disproportionate Three Gorges Dam. We will also go in Botswana with the Okavango, this unique river which flows into a desert forming the famous Okavango Delta. Vu du Ciel makes you discover once again another world, magnificent but threatened.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Differential relaxation

For example, when you're working in the office, your back and your arms are often extremely contracted when really only your eyes and hands are involved in the task.

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - France, Le Bruit de l'eau
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 France, Le Bruit de l'eau
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, we take you to “Le Bruit de l'eau”, an ecolodge nestled in the heart of the Baie de Somme nature reserve, in France. In the middle of nature, discover a place of absolute serenity inspired by the Japanese lifestyle and the travels of a great reporter, its founder.The intimate atmosphere that reigns at Le Bruit de l'eau (The Sound of Water) envelops you in a soothing calm, creating a cocoon made for relaxation and personal rejuvenation.This ecolodge allows us to cultivate awareness of the present moment and to open up to a fulfilling perception of our environment. Le Bruit de l'eau opens the doors to a transformative experience, where beauty of the natural...

programme.title: Reset - Season 1 - Sri Lanka, Santani
programme.desc: In this episode of Reset, we fly to Sri Lanka to discover an old tea plantation transformed into a sumptuous refuge: Santani.In order to find an alternative to the stress of urban life, the hotel offers a modern approach to Ayurveda. This traditional Indian medicine aims to establish a balance between the individual and the world around them. Thanks to the development of a personalized program, each visitor is invited to find harmony within this restorative sanctuary.Santani was created by a Sri Lankan eager to preserve our physical and spiritual well-being in a peaceful and healthy place to reconnect with the world differently. Let's discover a holistic approach to health, including...

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Atlantis
programme.desc: In this episode, our presenter Gabriela will visit Sustainable City in Dubai. She will discover how the residents garden, how they dress in sustainable clothes and, of course, how they eat by preparing healthy recipes. She will end her day with a Qigong class with a Qigong master.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The flavors of chocolate
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The flavors of chocolate
programme.desc: Today, the gluttons should not be disappointed since chocolate is in the spotlight. Discovered by the Mayans 2500 years ago and then more widely by the Aztecs, chocolate is today one of the favorite products in the world. And make no mistake about it: just as the kitchen has its great chefs, chocolate also has its great chocolatiers.As for recipes, Julie will introduce you to a cake that is only made in Saint Émilien and which combines chocolate with macarons, before heading to the Basque Country for a dessert to spice you up. We will then climb up the mountain to meet a Swiss cook. Finally, you will see that chocolate has several surprises in store for us: it has many virtues and is...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Hotel on Pacific
programme.desc: Val takes us to one of the most beautiful hotels in California: The Belmont Hotel! Much more than a hotel, this place is a natural haven where everything is made to relax while overlooking the Pacific. While relaxation is key, we also seek adventures, don’t we? While in this dream setting, we will practice yoga among the turtles, walk around the vertical and organic garden with the Chef and treat ourselves with a massage with essential oils coming directly from the garden.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Surfing & Vineyards
programme.desc: Yoga and Surf are two corner stones of the Californian spirit. In this episode, the best of both worlds meets as Val shares a surf session with a local champion from Malibu! What about the surfing outfit ? No need for it, there is a yoga mat. Let’s head to Ojai, in the middle of a vineyards, and joint the "Topa Wines" group for a yoga session at the sunset. Together, we will enjoy the "Pink moment": the time when Ojai’s mountains turn pink and your mind is blown away.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Repotting an indoor plant
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Repotting an indoor plant
programme.desc: We meet in the market garden of Romainville to learn how to take good care of our indoor plants. The plants that grow in our houses and flats are grown in pots. And when we buy a new plant, it is very important to repot it. The plants we buy come in pots that are often too small for them to grow well. That's why you put it in a bigger pot.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Agostina (Argentina)
programme.desc: When Agostina’s mother was diagnosed with breast cancer, she began seeking out soft natural fabrics that would not irritate her mother’s sensitive skin. She found it almost impossible to find the right materials, so she decided to make her own. Research led her to bamboo fibers and she realised she could make a positive impact on the environment, while developing sustainable and ethical fashion.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Solenn (Nantes)
programme.desc: A former school teacher reinvents herself when she discovers a passion for sharing other women’s stories. Always inspired by the journeys of strong women, Solenn began a blog to celebrate the women in her community. Solenn’s energy and passion fueled a dream of starting her own magazine. Thanks to a successful crowdfunding campaign, this dream blossomed into a professional career in journalism and she launched a print mook (magazine & book) called Etonnantes. Etonnantes features interviews of women of all backgrounds, celebrating their diversity and success in each field.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Sophie (Nantes)
programme.desc: Always an artist at heart, Sophie grew up in the countryside amongst nature and farm animals. Her love for creating art while respecting nature turned into a drive to seek out sustainable vegetable dyes.Today, her artwork and craft are intertwined as she makes beautiful prints and motifs that she colours with natural dyes, prepared from handpicked variety of flowers and plants that turn into stunning creations.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - So the great rivers may live - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 So the great rivers may live - Part 2
programme.desc: Yann Arthus-Bertrand thus continues his world tour and his inventory of the planet with the great rivers that suffer from man's excesses as a common thread. " So the great rivers may live" is the discovery of places, men and women who are fighting so that our waterways simply have their place on Earth. Thanks to his genius, man is correcting his mistakes: the Aral Sea, which once disappeared, is coming back. In New York, millions of people have drinking water thanks to the natural virtues of the forest.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Joint relaxation
programme.desc: The joints are the first parts of the body to react badly to stress, therefore it is essential to relax and relieve them as often as possible. Some ankle and knee rotations, combined with calm, controlled breathing will allow you to soothe your joints. With a few simple, well-targeted exercises, this session will bring you deep relaxation.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.desc: The call of the Forest is about discovering places but above all it is about meeting those who protect this priceless heritage. Forests are essential to mankind. They capture CO2, promote the formation of clouds and therefore rain, protect the soil from erosion and provide a habitat for wildlife. From Madagascar, to France, to Cameroon, or even Eritrea, Yann Arthus-Bertrand sets out to discover the most beautiful forests, those who live in them, those who protect them and those who live off them.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The cheese menu
programme.desc: Today, Julie sets on the roads of France to talk about an essential product on French tables: cheese. More than a gourmet craze, more than an end-of-meal treat, cheese is associated with French gastronomy, and is also recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. With nearly 1,000 varieties (soft cheeses, cooked, blue-veined, pressed cheeses…), the French are the biggest consumers of cheese in the world!

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Martial Arts and meditation
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Martial Arts and meditation
programme.desc: Today, we will meet in Santa Monica in the hub of the last healthy trends. Val will make us discover Erewhon, the biggest organic and hipster supermarket in Los Angeles. She will then meet Troy, a guru of healthy consciousness, for an outdoor class that mixes martial arts and meditation. Then, on the legendary "Muscle Beach", the beach of all athletes, we will meet David, an action movie double, for a very aerial workout.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.desc: Val takes us to discover Palm Springs, the trendy and designer city in the heart of the California desert. In the heart of a private collection, we will discover all the flora of this region: an outdoor plant museum, one of the largest collections of cacti, and even tequila flowers. Then we'll head to the Paséo Hotel, one of the zen and design hotels of Palm Springs, where we'll meet the chef of the restaurant who will concoct his signature dishes. We will end our day with a relaxing massage with crystals.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.desc: We meet again in the market garden of Romainville, for a special Halloween episode. In one of our first episodes, we learned how to sow a pumpkin seed. The pumpkins, which I had installed in my vegetable garden, have grown well now that we are in autumn. And that's good because today we're going to show you how to dig a pumpkin to make a beautiful Halloween lantern.
